news.title: Silan Announces New Generation of High-Efficiency IGBT Modules
news.summary: Silan Microelectronics launches new SGM series IGBT modules with improved efficiency and thermal performance for motor control and power conversion applications.
news.content:
Hangzhou, China - March 15, 2024 - Silan Microelectronics today announced the release of its new generation SGM series IGBT modules, featuring enhanced efficiency and improved thermal performance for demanding motor control and power conversion applications.

The new SGM series includes devices rated at 600V and 1200V with current capabilities ranging from 15A to 100A. Key improvements include reduced Vce(sat) by up to 15% compared to previous generations, resulting in lower conduction losses and higher system efficiency. The enhanced thermal design allows for operation at higher junction temperatures while maintaining reliability.

'Our new IGBT series represents a significant advancement in power semiconductor technology,' said Dr. Chen Wei, VP of Product Development at Silan. 'We've leveraged our vertically integrated manufacturing capabilities to optimize every aspect of device performance, from chip design to packaging.'

The SGM series is particularly well-suited for inverter air conditioners, washing machines, industrial motor drives, and new energy vehicle applications. Samples are available now with production quantities shipping in Q2 2024.

news.title: Silan Expands MEMS Sensor Portfolio with Ultra-Low Power Accelerometer
news.summary: New SC7A30 tri-axial accelerometer delivers industry-leading power consumption of just 100μA while maintaining high performance for wearable and IoT applications.
news.content:
Hangzhou, China - February 28, 2024 - Silan Microelectronics has expanded its MEMS sensor portfolio with the introduction of the SC7A30, an ultra-low power tri-axial accelerometer designed specifically for battery-powered wearable and IoT devices.

The SC7A30 achieves industry-leading power consumption of just 100μA in active mode at 100Hz output data rate, representing a 23% improvement over the previous generation. The device features 14-bit resolution and selectable measurement ranges of ±2g, ±4g, ±8g, and ±16g, making it suitable for a wide range of motion sensing applications.

'Battery life is a critical concern for wearable device manufacturers,' said Li Ming, Director of MEMS Product Line. 'The SC7A30's ultra-low power consumption enables our customers to extend device battery life from days to weeks without compromising performance.'

The sensor includes advanced features such as a 32-level FIFO, programmable interrupts for motion detection, and wake-on-motion capability that reduces power consumption to just 2μA in sleep mode. The SC7A30 is available in a compact 2x2mm LGA package and is sampling now.

news.title: Silan Reaches Milestone of 1 Billion MEMS Sensors Shipped
news.summary: Company celebrates major milestone as cumulative MEMS sensor shipments exceed 1 billion units, demonstrating strong market acceptance and manufacturing scale.
news.content:
Hangzhou, China - January 20, 2024 - Silan Microelectronics announced today that it has shipped over 1 billion MEMS sensors since entering the market in 2010, marking a significant milestone for the company's sensor business.

The achievement reflects Silan's successful strategy of leveraging its vertically integrated manufacturing capabilities to deliver high-quality, cost-competitive MEMS sensors for consumer electronics, automotive, and industrial applications. The company's MEMS portfolio includes accelerometers, gyroscopes, pressure sensors, and microphones.

'Reaching 1 billion units shipped is a testament to the trust our customers place in Silan's technology and quality,' said Wang Tao, Senior VP of Sales and Marketing. 'Our MEMS sensors are now used in hundreds of millions of smartphones, wearables, and IoT devices worldwide.'

Silan's MEMS manufacturing facility in Hangzhou operates 6-inch wafer lines with capacity exceeding 100 million units per month. The company continues to invest in expanding capacity and developing new sensor technologies including combo sensors and environmental sensors.

news.title: Silan Partners with Leading Home Appliance Manufacturer for Motor Control Solutions
news.summary: Strategic partnership to develop next-generation inverter motor control systems using Silan's IGBT and IPM products for energy-efficient appliances.
news.content:
Hangzhou, China - December 10, 2023 - Silan Microelectronics announced a strategic partnership with one of China's leading home appliance manufacturers to develop next-generation inverter motor control solutions for energy-efficient air conditioners, washing machines, and refrigerators.

The partnership will focus on developing optimized motor control systems using Silan's SGM series IGBTs and intelligent power modules (IPMs). The collaboration includes joint development of control algorithms, reference designs, and application-specific solutions tailored to the manufacturer's product requirements.

'We are excited to partner with Silan to bring more efficient and reliable motor control solutions to our products,' said the appliance manufacturer's CTO. 'Silan's power semiconductor expertise combined with their local support capabilities make them an ideal partner for our inverterization strategy.'

The partnership is expected to result in significant energy savings across the manufacturer's product lineup, with inverter models consuming 30-50% less energy than traditional on/off controlled appliances. Volume production is scheduled to begin in Q2 2024.

news.title: Silan Opens New 8-inch Wafer Fab to Meet Growing Demand
news.summary: New state-of-the-art 8-inch wafer fabrication facility increases production capacity by 50% to support growing demand for power semiconductors and MEMS sensors.
news.content:
Hangzhou, China - November 5, 2023 - Silan Microelectronics held the grand opening ceremony for its new 8-inch wafer fabrication facility, representing a major expansion of the company's manufacturing capabilities.

The new fab, constructed with an investment of approximately $500 million, features state-of-the-art equipment for power semiconductor and MEMS sensor manufacturing. The facility adds 50,000 wafers per month of capacity, increasing Silan's total output by approximately 50%.

'This expansion demonstrates our commitment to meeting the growing demand for power semiconductors and MEMS sensors in new energy, industrial, and consumer applications,' said Fan Weimin, Chairman and CEO of Silan. 'The new fab positions us to support our customers' growth for years to come.'

The facility incorporates advanced manufacturing technologies including copper metallization, thin wafer processing, and 3D packaging capabilities. It has already begun production ramp and is expected to reach full capacity by mid-2024.
